findings.message: The brief hard-codes an 'initial-load-only' loading flag, but the hook already has another catalog-hydration path: `handleStorefrontChangeImpl()` clears `products` before the `storefront`-driven `loadCatalog()` effect reruns (catalogConsoleActions.ts:140-175, useCatalogConsole.client.ts:207-215), so this plan would still show the same misleading empty state on storefront changes.

The constraints section tells implementers to use `bg-gate-border` for skeleton contrast, then line 48 says that utility does not exist in `globals.css`; this contradiction makes the implementation guidance unreliable and should be collapsed to one supported pattern.

The preferred inline `animate-pulse` approach drops the reduced-motion safeguard that the existing DS `Skeleton` already provides (packages/design-system/src/atoms/Skeleton.tsx:38); if inline skeletons remain the plan, the brief should explicitly require `motion-reduce:animate-none`.

note: Round 3 final — post-round fixes applied in-place to address both Major findings and Minor finding. Findings addressed: storefront-change hydration path added to isCatalogHydrating scope; bg-gate-border contradiction resolved to single pattern; motion-reduce:animate-none added to skeleton class guidance.
